sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Le Havre are in poor form, winless in their last 7 matches and failing to score in their last 5 games. In contrast, Angers are on a strong run with 3 wins in their last 5 matches, including back-to-back victories. This momentum gives Angers a clear edge heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between Le Havre and Angers have ended in draws (0-0 and 1-1), with both matches featuring fewer than 2.5 goals. Historically, Angers have not lost in their last 3 meetings against Le Havre, indicating a slight psychological advantage.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Le Havre struggle offensively, ranking 18th in goals scored (13) despite overperforming their expected goals, while conceding 22 goals. Angers, sitting 10th in the league with 22 points, have a balanced attack and defense, scoring 17 and conceding 18, which suggests they are the more consistent side.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Le Havre miss key contributors like Abdoulaye Touré and Fodé Doucouré, impacting their midfield stability. Angers, despite missing Himad Abdelli, have in-form players like Sidiki Cherif (4 goals) and a solid goalkeeper in Melvin Zinga (7.5 avg rating), potentially giving them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Le Havre's high foul count (3rd in the league with 226) and poor finishing (29 big chances missed) could expose them to Angers' counter-attacks. Angers’ ability to score late (7 goals after the 76th minute) might exploit Le Havre’s defensive lapses in the closing stages.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Le Havre’s scoring drought and Angers’ recent form, the away side looks more likely to take the points. Sofascore predicts a 40% chance of a home win, 25% draw, and 35% away win, but current trends lean slightly towards Angers securing a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Le Havre leads Angers 1-0 in a match played at Stade Oceane. The home side has shown dominance in key areas, taking the lead through a well-taken goal by Kenny Quetant in the 21st minute. Le Havre has controlled possession and created more threatening opportunities, while Angers has struggled to make an impact in the final third. The first half statistics reflect Le Havre's superiority in terms of entries into dangerous areas and creating big chances, though they missed a couple of notable opportunities to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: Le Havre has displayed consistent attacking intent throughout the first half, with multiple spells of sustained pressure, particularly around the time of their goal and towards the end of the half. Their momentum shifted positively in key moments, showcasing their ability to build play and threaten Angers' defense. On the other hand, Angers had a few brief periods of attacking momentum, especially in the middle of the half, but lacked the cutting edge to convert these moments into clear chances. Overall, Le Havre's attacking rhythm has been far more pronounced.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive solidity
halftimeSections.text: Defensively, Le Havre has held firm, with players like Ayumu Seko leading the way with crucial clearances and interceptions. Their backline has managed to restrict Angers to minimal penetration in dangerous zones, which is evident from the away side's lack of shots on target. Angers, meanwhile, has shown resilience in duels, winning a slightly higher percentage of aerial battles, but their defensive structure has been tested repeatedly by Le Havre's forward movements. Players like Jordan Lefort have made important contributions, but they’ve been under constant pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Kenny Quetant has been the standout for Le Havre, not only for his goal but also for his involvement in attacking plays, showing composure in front of goal despite missing a big chance. Loic Nego has also impressed with his defensive work rate and ability to win tackles. For Angers, Haris Belkebla has been a bright spot in midfield, winning tackles and attempting to link play, but the lack of support in the final third has limited his impact. Overall, Le Havre's players have outperformed their counterparts in most areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Le Havre is likely to maintain their attacking approach, seeking to capitalize on missed chances from the first half. With their dominance in possession and ability to create opportunities, they are well-positioned to add to their tally. Angers, however, will need to regroup and show more intent in attack if they are to get back into the game. Tactical adjustments might see them push higher up the pitch, but they’ll need to balance this with defensive caution against Le Havre's dangerous transitions. The home side appears to have the upper hand heading into the next 45 minutes.
